definition: A potential self-reinforcing doom loop where positive correlation → higher term premium → larger deficits (interest expense) → more long-end pressure → more positive correlation. Alternatively, a self-correcting mechanism where financial conditions tightening slows the economy and reduces deficits through automatic stabilizers.
evidence: Identified by generalist_01 as 'most important unanswered question'
Neither agent in any pair attempted to resolve this
Flagged in pair_0 debate as the critical gap
Direction of feedback loop has massive implications for regime stability
open_questions: Whether the loop is self-reinforcing (explosive) or self-correcting (stable)
Empirical tests to distinguish between doom loop and stabilization dynamics
Time horizon over which the feedback loop operates
